name: SB19
description:
'''SB19''' is a Filipino [[boy band]] formed in 2018, consisting of [[Josh Cullen (musician)|Josh]], [[Pablo (Filipino musician)|Pablo]], [[Stell (singer)|Stell]], [[Felip (musician)|Ken]], and [[Justin (Filipino musician)|Justin]]. They are noted for their contributions to the contemporary [[Pinoy pop]] scene and became the first Southeast Asian act to be nominated for a [[Billboard Music Awards|''Billboard'' Music Award]], as well as the first Southeast Asian act to make an appearance in the top 10 of the [[Billboard Social 50|''Billboard'' Social 50]]. 

SB19 is a Filipino boy group whose name carries layered meanings tied to both their musical ambition and their cross-cultural origins. The “SB” in SB19 was initially derived from their former Korean management company, ShowBT, but has since evolved to also represent “Sound Break,” symbolizing the group’s aim to break into the global music scene with a fresh sound, raise industry standards, and set new records. The number “19” reflects the connection between the Philippines and South Korea, the two countries central to the group’s formation. It has been interpreted as representing the numerical relationship between the two country codes, including their difference (82 − 63 = 19). Together, the name SB19 encapsulates both the group’s artistic mission and their unique cultural foundation.

Now a self-managed group under [[1Z Entertainment]], a company they established, SB19 members are actively involved in the creative process of their own music including songwriting, choreography, music video direction, and production. They have won numerous [[List of awards and nominations received by SB19|local and international awards]] throughout their career as Filipino musicians and performers. For their significant achievements in pioneering [[Philippine pop|Philippine pop music]] to the global scene, SB19 has been dubbed the "Kings of P-pop" by the public and various media. The group promotes their music as part of [[Pinoy pop]], a sub-genre of [[Music of the Philippines#Popular music|Original Pilipino Music]].

In 2025, following the release of their anticipated ''[[Simula at Wakas|Simula at Wakas EP]]'', SB19 became the first Filipino act to hold and sell out two nights at the [[Philippine Arena]] with the kickoff to their ''[[Simula at Wakas World Tour]]''. The succeeding international tour included stops all over North America, Asia, Middle East and Oceania. The EP garnered chart success both locally and internationally. As of January 2026, the group has amassed more than one billion streams on Spotify across all song credits.
